basics.name: Seungjoo Han
basics.label: Process Development Engineer
basics.summary: A dedicated chemical engineer with a strong background in characterization and thin film deposition using ALD. Holding a master's degree from Konkuk University and several years of hands-on experience in research and industry, with a robust skill set in material analysis, IP administration, and project management. SCI-indexed publications and multiple recognitions for research contributions. Passionate about innovation and continuous learning.

work.name: Wonik IPS
work.summary: Low resistance memory word-line (W/L) metal process development.
highlights: Development of high-throughput, low-resistivity metal ALD processes for memory W/L applications

work.name: ASM
work.summary: Overseeing metal ALD processes for memory applications (DRAM/VNAND), with supporting roles in Logic foundry and advanced-node demonstrations.
highlights: DRAM: Implemented low-resistance W/L metal ALD process for BCAT, VCT, and 3D DRAM architectures
VNAND: Executed low-resistance W/L metal ALD process to substitute the established W/L layer
Supported metal ALD process development for the BSPDN layer of Logic devices
Provided HVM (High-Volume Manufacturing) issue support for logic foundry customers
Supported C&F (Characterization & Feasibility) demonstrations for advanced-node memory devices
Received 2 Spot Awards (Aug 2022, Mar 2023) for research and field engineering contributions

work.name: EM-Tech
work.summary: Multi-disciplinary role covering R&D, analytical chemistry, and IP administration for aerosol device and sound-absorption material development.
highlights: R&D: Developed e-cigarette devices, gel-liquid formulations, and sound absorption materials
Analysis: Performed quantitative nicotine-delivery analysis using GC/FID
IP: Conducted patent analytics and prior-art research for R&D projects
IP: Managed monthly IP expenses and quarterly job-invention reward settlements

courses: Characterization of Organic Nano Thin Film
highlights: Advisor: Prof. Yo-Sep Min
Research topic: Characterization and Growth of ZnO Thin Films by Atomic Layer Deposition
Investigated 1,5-pentanediol as an oxygen precursor for the ALD of ZnO thin films
Coursework in semiconductor device fundamentals and instrumental analysis
Working as an undergraduate research assistant

awards.title: Spot Award
awards.summary: Aggressively supported film resistivity issue in DRAM Mo JDP and solved the mismatch

awards.title: Spot Award
awards.summary: Fully supported Mo JDP tool to solve the HW & Process problems

publications.name: 1,5-pentanediol as an oxygen precursor for atomic layer deposition of zinc oxide thin films
publications.summary: First-author SCI publication demonstrating 1,5-pentanediol as a novel oxygen precursor for ALD of ZnO thin films.

publications.name: Highly uniform and vertically aligned SnO2 nanochannel arrays for photovoltaic applications
Novel chemical route for atomic layer deposition of MoS2 thin film on SiO2/Si substrate

projects.name: ZnO Thin Film ALD with 1,5-pentanediol (M.S. Thesis)
projects.summary: Investigated the use of 1,5-pentanediol as a novel oxygen precursor for the atomic layer deposition of ZnO thin films, covering film growth characteristics, structural properties, and electrical performance.
highlights: First-author SCI publication in Chemistry of Materials (2017)
Surface/structural characterization (HR-TEM, GIXRD, XPS)

relavant coursework.name: Thin Film Engineering (2015)
highlights: Vacuum deposition techniques, structure, properties, and applications of thin films.

relavant coursework.name: Characterization of Organic Nano Thin Film (2014)
highlights: Analytical methods for organic materials and thin films, including vacuum coating techniques.

relavant coursework.name: Engineering of Semiconductor Devices (2014)
highlights: Semiconductor physics, device operating principles, and fabrication processes.

relavant coursework.name: Advanced Instrumental Analyses (2014)
highlights: Principles of analytical methods, sample preparation, and data interpretation.

relavant coursework.name: Solid State Chemistry (2013)
highlights: Crystal structure, atomic bonding, crystal defects, phase diagrams, and synthesis.
